Why TypeScript

Published on January 15, 2025

TypeScript has emerged as a powerful tool for developers looking to enhance their JavaScript experience. By introducing static typing, TypeScript helps catch errors at compile time rather than runtime, leading to more robust and maintainable code. This feature is particularly beneficial in large codebases where understanding the flow of data can become complex.

Moreover, TypeScript's compatibility with existing JavaScript code allows developers to gradually adopt it without needing to rewrite their entire codebase. With features like interfaces, enums, and generics, TypeScript not only improves code quality but also enhances developer productivity by providing better tooling and autocompletion in modern IDEs.
